project.name: ImageProcessingScoringAPI
project.description: This example uses the Event Stream Processing Scoring API to process image data via synchronous request-responses for a multi-stage pipeline utilising Connected Component Labeling (CCL) to detect objects obstructing a fire exit with no prior training.

window-python.name: w_background_subtract
window-python.description: Loads the static background image from disk and subtracts it from each incoming frame to suppress fixed-scene clutter before further processing.
window-python.code:
import os import cv2 import numpy as np import esp_utils
_BG = None
def _load_background(): global _BG if _BG is None: path = os.path.join(os.environ.get("ESP_PROJECT_HOME", ""), "test_files", "static.jpg") _BG = cv2.imread(path).astype(np.float32) return _BG
def subtract_background(data, context):
frame = esp_utils.image_conversion.blob_image_to_opencv_image(data["image"]) bg = _load_background()
if frame.shape != bg.shape: bg = cv2.resize(bg, (frame.shape[1], frame.shape[0]))
frame_hsv = cv2.cvtColor(frame, cv2.COLOR_BGR2HSV).astype(np.float32) bg_hsv = cv2.cvtColor(bg.astype(np.uint8), cv2.COLOR_BGR2HSV).astype(np.float32)
dH = np.abs(frame_hsv[:,:,0] - bg_hsv[:,:,0]) dS = np.abs(frame_hsv[:,:,1] - bg_hsv[:,:,1]) dV = np.abs(frame_hsv[:,:,2] - bg_hsv[:,:,2])
diff = (dH * 1.5 + dS * 1.2 + dV * 0.3) diff = np.clip(diff, 0, 255).astype(np.uint8)
# Helps remove JPEG noise diff[diff < 25] = 0
kernel = cv2.getStructuringElement(cv2.MORPH_ELLIPSE, (9, 9)) diff = cv2.morphologyEx(diff, cv2.MORPH_OPEN, kernel)
diff = cv2.cvtColor(diff, cv2.COLOR_GRAY2BGR)
event = {} event["subtracted_image"] = esp_utils.image_conversion.opencv_image_to_blob_image(diff)
return event

window-calculate.name: w_contrast
window-calculate.description: This window uses contrast stretching to expand the range of pixel intensities in the grayscale iamges. The goal is to make objects stand out from the background.

window-calculate.name: w_blur
window-calculate.description: This window runs Gaussian convolution over the contrast images to smooth the gradients out so that we don't over-segment objects in the foreground.

window-calculate.name: w_thresh
window-calculate.description: This window turns the constrast maps into binary masks, essentially deciding which pixels will be included in the CCL segmentation. i.e. imagine a text document, thresholding will determine which pixels are text (1s) and which are not (0s)

window-python.name: w_annotate
window-python.description: Converts the CCL label map so each object ID has a unique colour in the visualisation image, and also draws the same bounding boxes onto the original input frame. Objects mostly outside the fire exit ROI are filtered out.
window-python.code:
import numpy as np import cv2 import esp_utils
np.random.seed(42)
ROI = (730, 10, 1230, 1050) THRESH = 0.3
def _parse_list(val): if val is None or val == "": return [] if isinstance(val, (list, tuple, np.ndarray)): return [float(x) for x in val] return [float(x) for x in val.split(",")]
def _bbox_inside_ratio(x1, y1, x2, y2, roi): rx1, ry1, rx2, ry2 = roi ix1 = max(x1, rx1) iy1 = max(y1, ry1) ix2 = min(x2, rx2) iy2 = min(y2, ry2) inter_area = max(0, ix2 - ix1) * max(0, iy2 - iy1) box_area = max(1, (x2 - x1) * (y2 - y1)) return inter_area / box_area
def _draw_boxes(canvas, xmins, xmaxs, ymins, ymaxs, ids): num_boxes = min(len(xmins), len(xmaxs), len(ymins), len(ymaxs)) for i in range(num_boxes): x1 = int(xmins[i]) x2 = int(xmaxs[i]) y1 = int(ymins[i]) y2 = int(ymaxs[i]) color = tuple(np.random.randint(0, 255, 3).tolist()) cv2.rectangle(canvas, (x1, y1), (x2, y2), color, 2) if i < len(ids): cv2.putText(canvas, f"ID: {int(ids[i])}", (x1, max(y1 - 5, 0)), cv2.FONT_HERSHEY_SIMPLEX, 0.5, color, 1, cv2.LINE_AA)
def colorize_event(data, context):
event = {}
label_map = esp_utils.image_conversion.blob_image_to_opencv_image(data["outputImage"])
if len(label_map.shape) == 3: label_map = cv2.cvtColor(label_map, cv2.COLOR_BGR2GRAY)
raw = esp_utils.image_conversion.blob_image_to_opencv_image(data["image"]) raw = raw.copy()
xmins_all = _parse_list(data["xMinListOut"]) xmaxs_all = _parse_list(data["xMaxListOut"]) ymins_all = _parse_list(data["yMinListOut"]) ymaxs_all = _parse_list(data["yMaxListOut"]) ids_all = _parse_list(data["idListOut"])
xmins, xmaxs, ymins, ymaxs, ids = [], [], [], [], []
for i in range(min(len(xmins_all), len(xmaxs_all), len(ymins_all), len(ymaxs_all))): x1 = int(xmins_all[i]) x2 = int(xmaxs_all[i]) y1 = int(ymins_all[i]) y2 = int(ymaxs_all[i])
if _bbox_inside_ratio(x1, y1, x2, y2, ROI) >= THRESH: xmins.append(x1) xmaxs.append(x2) ymins.append(y1) ymaxs.append(y2) if i < len(ids_all): ids.append(int(ids_all[i]))
valid_ids = set(ids) filtered_label_map = np.zeros_like(label_map)
for obj_id in valid_ids: filtered_label_map[label_map == obj_id] = obj_id
label_map = filtered_label_map
label_map_norm = cv2.normalize(label_map, None, 0, 255, cv2.NORM_MINMAX).astype(np.uint8) vis = cv2.applyColorMap(label_map_norm, cv2.COLORMAP_TURBO)
_draw_boxes(vis, xmins, xmaxs, ymins, ymaxs, ids)
_draw_boxes(raw, xmins, xmaxs, ymins, ymaxs, ids)
rx1, ry1, rx2, ry2 = ROI cv2.rectangle(vis, (rx1, ry1), (rx2, ry2), (0, 255, 0), 2)
fg_mask = (label_map > 0).astype(np.uint8) fg_mask_3 = np.stack([fg_mask]*3, axis=-1)
overlay = raw.copy() blended = cv2.addWeighted(raw, 0.6, vis, 0.4, 0) overlay[fg_mask_3 == 1] = blended[fg_mask_3 == 1]
event["visImage"] = esp_utils.image_conversion.opencv_image_to_blob_image(vis) event["rawImage"] = esp_utils.image_conversion.opencv_image_to_blob_image(raw) event["overlayImage"] = esp_utils.image_conversion.opencv_image_to_blob_image(overlay)
return event

window-calculate.name: w_ccl
window-calculate.description: This window takes the binary image and groups connected foreground pixels into distinct labeled objects. It outputs a label map image along with metadata such as object counts and bounding box coordinates for each detected object.

window-calculate.name: w_gray
window-calculate.description: Takes input images and converts them into single channel grayscale images.

window-python.name: w_fire_exit_filter
window-python.description: Filters CCL detections to only objects intersecting the fire exit region, emits one event per object, and saves each cropped region to scoring_outputs/.
window-python.code:
import os import cv2 import numpy as np import esp_utils
def _parse_list(val): if val is None or val == "": return [] if isinstance(val, (list, tuple)): return [float(x) for x in val] return [float(x) for x in val.split(",")]
def intersects(box, roi): bx1, bx2, by1, by2 = box rx1, rx2, ry1, ry2 = roi return not ( bx2 < rx1 or bx1 > rx2 or by2 < ry1 or by1 > ry2 )
def process(data, context):
events = []
ROI = (733, 1250, 10, 1050)
xmins = _parse_list(data["xMinListOut"]) xmaxs = _parse_list(data["xMaxListOut"]) ymins = _parse_list(data["yMinListOut"]) ymaxs = _parse_list(data["yMaxListOut"]) ids = _parse_list(data["idListOut"])
n = min(len(xmins), len(xmaxs), len(ymins), len(ymaxs))
frame = None
for i in range(n):
box = ( int(xmins[i]), int(xmaxs[i]), int(ymins[i]), int(ymaxs[i]) )
if not intersects(box, ROI): continue
object_id = int(ids[i]) if i < len(ids) else i
event = { "index": data["index"], "object_id": object_id, "x_min": box[0], "x_max": box[1], "y_min": box[2], "y_max": box[3], } events.append(event)
return events

window-python.name: w_thresh_viz
window-python.description: Stretches the binary mask to full 0-255 range for visualisation, making foreground pixels white and background pixels black.
window-python.code:
import numpy as np import cv2 import esp_utils
def visualize(data, context):
img = esp_utils.image_conversion.blob_image_to_opencv_image( data["binary_image"] )
if len(img.shape) == 3: img = cv2.cvtColor(img, cv2.COLOR_BGR2GRAY)
# Any non-zero pixel becomes 255 for preview _, stretched = cv2.threshold(img, 0, 255, cv2.THRESH_BINARY)
stretched_bgr = cv2.cvtColor(stretched, cv2.COLOR_GRAY2BGR)
event = {} event["viz_image"] = esp_utils.image_conversion.opencv_image_to_blob_image(stretched_bgr)
return event
